title: Longest Balanced Substring After One Swap
performanceTarget: top 1% execution time and overall performance
note: C++ proposals for this challenge. All three variants are in this folder because C++ is the best fit for the runtime and memory trade-offs of this prefix-balance problem.

variants.languageReason: C++ provides contiguous arrays, low branch overhead, and no GC/runtime penalty; the prefix-bucket solution stays O(n) while keeping the code auditable and memory linear.
variants.approachJustification: Prefix-balance buckets over [-n, n], same-balance windows for no swap, and monotone per-bucket pointers for +2/-2 repairable windows under outside-character length caps.

variants.languageReason: Flat integer arrays and inline helper functions minimize allocation and lookup overhead on LeetCode's C++ runner, which is ideal for a branch-light linear scan.
variants.approachJustification: Intrusive per-balance queues with lazy expiration; each prefix index is pushed and expired at most once per repair direction.

variants.languageReason: A single vector of prefix pairs sorted in place keeps auxiliary memory compact while retaining C++'s inlined sort and low runtime overhead.
variants.approachJustification: Sort prefix (balance, index) pairs, process equal-balance groups for balanced windows, and process balance-difference-2 groups with two pointers for one-swap repair caps.

reasoningSummary: One useful swap changes a chosen window's #1-#0 balance by exactly 2, so only balances 0, +2, and -2 matter. Prefix balances find these windows; outside-character availability reduces to length caps of 2*total0 or 2*total1.
timeComplexity: O(n) recommended and runtime / O(n log n) memory
